## Frequently Asked Questions

### What is the best Aura alternative?

LifeLock is the most well-known alternative with up to $3M insurance and Norton antivirus bundle. However, Aura found more dark web exposures, sent alerts faster, and includes three-bureau monitoring on all plans — while LifeLock locks this behind its highest tier.

### Is LifeLock better than Aura?

No, based on head-to-head testing. Aura found more dark web exposures, sent fraud alerts faster, and includes three-bureau credit monitoring on every plan. LifeLock is better only if you specifically want the Norton antivirus and VPN bundle.

results.title: Best Identity Theft Protection Services We've Tested in 2026 - CNET
results.content:
Note: On March 19, Aura disclosed a security incident related to a highly targeted phishing attack which resulted in the leak of contact information for approximately 35,000 current and former subscribers. The company has since reported that "No sensitive information provided by customers to Aura for monitoring purposes -- such as Social Security numbers, financial information, credit records, or passwords -- was compromised."

Due to the limited scope of the breach, Aura's swift response and the fact that monitored data was not affected, CNET is maintaining its recommendation of Aura at this time. However, if you're looking to move to a different service, we recommend LifeLock and IdentityForce as alternatives.

Why we like it: [...] Who it's best for:

LifeLock is a good pick if you want peace of mind and don't mind paying extra for robust coverage. It's also a good service if you want identity theft insurance for your children. LifeLock's family plans uniquely offer identity theft insurance to minors, which even our best overall service Aura doesn't. Families can receive up to $25,000 for stolen funds and personal expenses reimbursement per child in addition to $1 million to cover lawyers and experts necessary to recover their identity.

Who shouldn't sign up for it:

If you're looking for a budget-friendly identity theft solution or if you want antivirus protection, a VPN or a password manager for no extra cost, look elsewhere. LifeLock offers all three, but you'll pay a pretty penny for them. [...] You can potentially receive more identity theft protection insurance through IdentityForce than Aura ($2 million versus $1 million per adult). IdentityForce's top family plan is also cheaper than Aura's. However, be sure to note that IdentityForce's family plans cover two adults while Aura covers up to five. Both cover unlimited children.

Among its top features, IdentityForce provides a password manager, virtual private network antivirus protection, investment account alerts and social media monitoring.

Unique to IdentityForce is a personal identity safety score that's used to create a personalized action plan. Its comprehensive quarterly credit reports also stood out to us in our tests, though it's worth noting, you don't have to pay money to access your credit reports.

results.title: Aura vs LifeLock Comparison for 2026 | Security.org
results.content:
Pro Tip: Some identity theft protection services offer more robust dark web monitoring than others. It depends how many sites each one digs through on the dark web. Take a look at our list of the best identity theft protection with dark web monitoring for our favorites.

LifeLock takes a preventative approach. When it finds your information, it makes it easy for you to file requests to take these details down. Aura is more reactive. It only provides alerts that a breach has occurred, but it’s up to you to take action on your own. [...] Keep reading to learn more.

## About LifeLock

Lifelock Dashboard

Lifelock Dashboard

LifeLock was founded in 2005. It’s now part of Gen Digital, the same company that owns the antivirus brands Norton, Avira, and AVG. As such, LifeLock excels at offering cybersecurity suites that help in preventing digital identity theft.

LifeLock has a partnership with Norton in particular, offering identity theft protection bundles with a Norton 360 digital security subscription. That’s not an uncommon partnership, but unlike Aura, these bundles are optional. That means you can just grab LifeLock if you already have cybersecurity tools, or you can bundle identity and digital protection and save money.

>> Learn More: LifeLock Review 2026

## About Aura [...] If you need to access insurance, Aura and LifeLock have you covered. However, the amounts are significantly different. Aura will cover each adult up to $1 million, whereas LifeLock is up to $3 million on its premium tiers. It breaks that $3 million down to coverage for lawyers, experts, and stolen funds reimbursements.

Verdict: LifeLock is the clear winner in this category if you’re willing to pay for a pricier subscription. Identity restoration is a crucial safety net, and that insurance coverage that goes up to $3 million is much better than what Aura offers. That said, if you’re aiming for a budget-friendly option, Aura offers better value at its price point.
